description_long: This object is a small bird with a slender, elongated body and a long, thin, slightly curved beak, characteristic of nectar-feeding species. Its plumage is predominantly dark, with iridescent blue and green hues along the head and upper body, and a distinct white band running across the upper chest and neck. The wings are tucked close to the body, and the tail is short and slightly pointed. The eyes are small and positioned laterally, giving the bird a streamlined appearance. The legs are not visible, suggesting they are either tucked beneath the body or very short. The overall form and coloration suggest a bird adapted for agile flight and feeding on flowers, commonly seen in urban parks and gardens.
description: A slender, iridescent bird with a long, curved beak, dark blue-green plumage, and a white neck band, resembling a nectar-feeder. Its streamlined body and short, pointed tail suggest agility and speed.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's long, curved beak, iridescent blue-green head, and white neck band.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tapered, pointed tail and the white band encircling the upper body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same elongated beak and iridescent plumage, with the body profile visible.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the narrow, pointed beak, rounded head, and the symmetry of the white neck band.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Iridescent blue and green with a solid white neck band and darker underparts
